description.task: Implement a JvmMemoryAnalyzer class that simulates JVM memory monitoring. Track heap usage over time, detect potential memory leaks (steadily increasing usage without GC relief), and report GC efficiency based on pre/post-GC heap snapshots.
instructions: averageUsagePercent() is already implemented — do not change it
isPotentialMemoryLeak() finds the last 3 snapshots where afterGc=false and checks if usedMb is strictly increasing
isPotentialMemoryLeak() returns false if fewer than 3 non-GC snapshots exist
gcEfficiency() finds pairs where snapshot[i].afterGc=false and snapshot[i+1].afterGc=true
For each pair, compute (pre.usedMb - post.usedMb) / pre.usedMb * 100; return the average
description.hint: For isPotentialMemoryLeak(): filter non-GC snapshots, take last 3, check s[0].usedMb < s[1].usedMb && s[1].usedMb < s[2].usedMb. For gcEfficiency(): iterate consecutive pairs checking afterGc transitions.
description.expectedOutput:
Avg usage: 56.5%
Leak detected: true
GC efficiency: 68.4%

description.task: Build a ConnectionPoolMonitor class that tracks database connection pool performance metrics. Monitor wait time for acquiring connections, active vs idle connection counts, and detect pool exhaustion scenarios where all connections are in use.
instructions: averageWaitTimeMs() is already implemented — do not change it
activeConnectionsAt() counts events where acquireTimeMs <= timestampMs < releaseTimeMs
wasPoolExhaustedAt() returns true when activeConnectionsAt(timestampMs) == poolSize
getSlowAcquisitions() filters events where waitTimeMs > thresholdMs and sorts by waitTimeMs descending
All methods must work correctly on an empty events list
description.hint: activeConnectionsAt(): events.stream().filter(e -> e.acquireTimeMs() <= ts && ts < e.releaseTimeMs()).count(). getSlowAcquisitions(): filter waitTimeMs > threshold, sort with Comparator.comparingLong(ConnectionEvent::waitTimeMs).reversed().
description.expectedOutput:
Avg wait: 67.5ms
Active at t=300: 3
Pool exhausted at t=200: true
Slow (>20ms): 1

description.task: Implement an LruCache class (Least Recently Used eviction) that tracks cache hit rates. The cache has a fixed capacity; when full, it evicts the least recently accessed entry. Report hit/miss statistics and identify the most accessed keys.
instructions: The LinkedHashMap with accessOrder=true and removeEldestEntry override is already set up
get() increments hits when key exists (returns non-null), misses otherwise
hitRate() returns hits / (hits + misses) * 100.0, returning 0.0 when no accesses have occurred
containsKey() checks presence without affecting hits/misses counters
description.hint: The LRU eviction policy is handled by the LinkedHashMap subclass. get() just needs: V value = cache.get(key); if (value != null) hits++; else misses++; return value. The LinkedHashMap access order automatically promotes accessed keys.
description.expectedOutput:
Hit rate: 66.7%
Contains b: false
Contains a: true
Size: 3
